normalized_content: GitHub issue: Feature request: memory flush before /new and /restart session reset URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/41216 Number: 41216 State: closed State reason: completed Created at: 2026-03-09T15:59:15Z Updated at: 2026-04-27T04:29:30Z Closed at: 2026-04-24T21:05:54Z Labels: Body: ## Problem `/new` and `/restart` immediately destroy the session without any opportunity to persist context. The pre-compaction memory flush (`compaction.memoryFlush`) works great for auto-compaction, but session resets bypass it entirely. This means any work, decisions, or context accumulated since the last memory write is silently lost. ## Proposed solution Add a config option like `compaction.flushOnSessionReset: true` (or `memoryFlush.onReset: true`) that triggers the same silent memory flush turn before `/new` or `/restart` clears the session. The flow would be: 1. User sends `/new` 2. Gateway fires the memory flush prompt (same as pre-compaction flush) 3. Agent writes durable notes to memory files 4. Session is cleared ## Why this matters For users with persistent memory systems (memory/*.md files, MEMORY.md, etc.), the pre-compaction flush is the safety net that prevents context loss. But `/new` and `/restart` have no equivalent safety net, so users must manually remember to say "write session notes" before resetting — which they often forget. ## Current workaround Manually asking the agent to write session notes before running `/new`. Works but relies on human memory, which is ironic for a memory management feature. ## Environment - OpenClaw 2026.3.8 - Running on Linux (LXC), Telegram channel - Using tiered memory system with domain-specific files
title: Feature request: memory flush before /new and /restart session reset
